text:
On Novig, a limit order lets you set the price you're willing to accept — it waits in the market until someone matches it at that price or better, or it expires. A market order executes immediately against available offers at the best prices currently available.

Use a limit order when you're willing to wait for a specific price. Use a market order when you want to fill right away and don't mind taking the current best offer.

Since Novig is a peer-to-peer exchange, your order is matched against other users' orders, not against a house. The mechanics of how orders are prioritized and matched — we can have someone walk you through those specifics if you want.

Novig Support
